Code fragment: public final TableField<UsersRecord, Integer> USER_ID = createField("user_id", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The Users table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (user_id) even though a natural unique key exists on the LOGIN column, which is immutable by nature and could serve as a primary key. Consider using LOGIN as the primary key instead, or if user_id is needed for foreign key simplicity, document why the synthetic key is necessary.

Code fragment: public final TableField<UsersRecord, UsersRole> ROLE = createField("role", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.VARCHAR(5).nullable(false).asEnumDataType(com.devproserv.courses.jooq.enums.UsersRole.class), this, "");
Explanation: The ROLE column uses an ENUM type to restrict allowed values, which makes adding or removing roles difficult without schema changes. Consider creating a separate 'roles' lookup table and referencing it with a foreign key instead.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the COURSES table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and cause maintenance issues if the table schema changes. Replace with explicit column selection like `ctx.select(Courses.COURSES.COURSE_ID, Courses.COURSES.NAME, Courses.COURSES.DESCRIPTION)`.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the Users table, which is inefficient when only checking for existence. Replace with `ctx.fetchExists(selectOne().from(Users.USERS).where(...))` or select only the specific column needed like `ctx.select(Users.USERS.USER_ID)`.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the USERS table, which wastes bandwidth and memory. Specify only the required columns explicitly, such as `ctx.select(Users.USERS.LOGIN, Users.USERS.ROLE).from(Users.USERS)`.
